news.title: Meta jumps into AI coding market in effort to chase Anthropic and OpenAI
news.summary: Meta is upgrading its Muse Spark artificial intelligence model under the leadership of AI chief Alexandr Wang. The new model will be called Muse Spark and will be available to all Meta users by the end of 2014.

news.title: June home sales disappoint as prices reach an all-time high
news.summary: Home sales dropped in June month over month as mortgage rates remain stubbornly high. Prices hit an all-time high. Home sales were down in June, but up in May and April.

news.title: Micron shares rise 7% after announcing billions more in U.S. chipmaking investments
news.summary: Micron announced a new round of investments aimed at boosting the U.S. semiconductor supply chain. Micron: The investments will be made in memory, storage, logic and other areas.

news.title: Oil prices fall as market bets U.S.-Iran fighting won't escalate into wider war
news.summary: Oil prices turned lower on Thursday after the U.S. carried out fresh strikes on Iran. Renewed concerns about supply disruptions in the Middle East.

news.title: Trump can halt trade with Spain using law behind scrapped tariffs: Greer
news.summary: Trump asserted that IEEPA authorized him to slap tariffs on nearly every country on earth. Supreme Court struck down those import duties. Trump also claimed that he could impose tariffs on virtually every country in the world.

news.title: Only 5% of U.S. adults can ace this 8-question financial-literacy test. Can you?
news.summary: Financial literacy is at a 10-year low, according to a new report. The report says the drop in financial literacy is devastating bank accounts.

news.title: The dot-com crash was a $5 trillion blip. Why the next financial crisis could hit 4 times harder.
news.summary: The next global financial crisis is already under way. Here is how it will unfold. The next financial crisis will be triggered by the next global recession.

news.title: ‘I’ve plenty of time on my hands’: Advisers bombard me with offers of free steak dinners. Is it wrong to go for the food?
news.summary: “I get hungry for a meal at a good restaurant,” he says. “I’m a big fan of good food.”

news.title: PepsiCo cut snack prices again, but not enough to get American consumers to buy more
news.summary: PepsiCo’s North America business lagged in the latest quarter, and the stock was having its worst day in 15 months. Strength in international business led to an earnings beat.

news.title: Did you mess up on your taxes? The IRS just made it easier to avoid paying a penalty.
news.summary: . It’s a ‘major taxpayer win,’ according to one advocate for taxpayers. ‘Major taxpayer win’ says another.
